title: Being Broke Costs $1,300/Year More Than Being Rich
hook: Low-income Americans pay up to $1,300 extra a year for the exact same life — and the system designed it that way.
angle: The poverty premium is a documented economic trap: food, banking, credit, and insurance all cost more when you're poor, creating a cycle that's hard to escape.
audience: Low-income earners, anyone living paycheck to paycheck, people frustrated with financial systems working against them
key_numbers: 43% more per ounce on food without bulk buying
$600/year in banking fees for unbanked Americans
400% APR on payday loans vs 3% at a bank
$500/year extra car insurance for low-income zip codes
